paragraphs.text: From Global Brand Story to China Growth Strategy
Golf participation is growing, and indoor venues are making access more repeatable.
Comparable on-course data shows steady growth, while indoor rounds are scaling much faster — expanding golf from occasional course participation into more urban and accessible play occasions.
Traditional golf participation continues to grow steadily.
The core golf base remains meaningful — but growth is steady rather than explosive.
Urban venues are turning golf into a more frequent and lower-threshold experience.
Indoor venues are becoming a practical entry engine for new players, social occasions and repeat participation.
Strategic interpretation: golf access is shifting from exclusive course occasions toward a broader urban participation funnel.
Sources: Ping An Securities / Huajing Industry Research Institute; Guanyan Tianxia; China Golf Association / China Sports Daily.
Golf access is moving from “exclusive course participation” toward a broader urban participation funnel.
Golfcore is making golf discoverable before consumers become players.
China Golf Market Observation: Social Interest & Youth Signal
For many young consumers, the first entry into golf may be style, content and social identity — before full participation in the sport.
views on Xiaohongshu golf outfit-related terms
Golf style is already breaking beyond core players and entering platform culture.
Sources: Xinhua / Beijing Business Today; Xiaohongshu Ball Sports Trend Forecast; Future Market Insights.
Malbon can convert golfcore attention into cultural entry — from wearing the code to joining the game.

Xiaohongshu Search Growth
Y24 vs Y25

Douyin Search Growth
Y24 vs Y25
Users aged 23–35 account for over 50% of the golf-interest audience
Although golf has gained more visibility, it still feels difficult to enter and lacks appeal for new users.
The market shift creates a contradiction: young consumers are attracted by golf’s lifestyle appeal but still feel unsure if they belong in golf culture.
72% of users believe that the high cost of golf equipment and club memberships is not only a basic requirement to get into the sport, but also a way people show social status and identity*
Source: CBNData’s 2025 Sports & Outdoor Interest Group Consumer Insights Paper, N=1,500
“I really wish there were more good-looking pants. The ones in stores are either too short or just not nice…, and the skirts aren’t great either.”
“Everything is just white and blue now, maybe a slightly brighter yellow at best. The colors and patterns are all so boring. I can’t even find anything in neon colors that I want to buy.”
Unlike F1 or tennis, it doesn’t have strong cultural icons that break into popularity
Malbon has seen a significant increase in conversation volume within the golf category, with further growth potential in the lifestyle space.
Number of RED Mentions and YTD Growth Rate
Relevant on and off the course; from fairway to city life.
Not followers, but fellow players connected by a shared culture.
Malbon was not built to simply make golf apparel. It was built to make golf feel fresh, welcoming and culturally alive for today’s generation.
It respects the game but does not let old codes decide who belongs. It gives players and newcomers permission to participate in golf culture in their own way.
Malbon can make golf easier to enter without making it less aspirational.
The opportunity is not to become a more professional golf brand, or a more golf-looking lifestyle brand — but to own the space between golf credibility and lifestyle entry.
Professional and credible, but often formal, technical and distant.
Stylish and culturally relevant, but often only borrowing golf aesthetics.
The cultural entry point into golf lifestyle in China.
「城市果岭 风格无界」Turns golf-rooted identity into a lifestyle community.
Malbon starts from the green, expands into city lifestyle, invites people through style and cultural expression, then gathers them into a community asset.
Malbon’s credibility starts from the game — its rituals, mindset, self-challenge and connection.
The city extends the green beyond the course, making golf part of stores, coffee, travel, indoor golf and social moments.
Product design, golfcore appeal and collaborations with art, music and fashion invite more people into Malbon’s world.
Malbon gathers people who like the product, believe in the attitude and are curious to participate — turning shared taste into brand belonging.
Social, campaign and offline should work together to move people from curiosity to participation, then from participation to belonging.
Build a diverse content system around golf spirit, lifestyle moments, styling-led expression and community participation — making Malbon easier to understand and closer to daily life.
Use hero campaigns, KOL stories and paid amplification to turn “Urban Green, Boundless Style” into high-impact narratives that reach beyond core golfers and build lifestyle aspiration.
Transform stores, ranges, indoor golf, pop-ups and city gatherings into accessible entry points where people experience golf culture, meet fellow players and return as part of the community.
We are not targeting “golfers” only — we are building Malbon’s China world.
Real golfers who validate Malbon’s golf credibility.
Golfcore / lifestyle consumers entering through dressing and social content.
Artists, photographers, buyers, coffee people, musicians and creators.
Coaches, local KOCs, range hosts and golf community organizers.
Women attracted by style, experience and beginner-friendly entry points.
Each audience plays a different role in making Malbon understood, desired and joined.
Malbon’s brand accounts are visually strong but need a clearer content structure to drive discovery and engagement.
WeChat has richer SVG interactions and editorial layouts; RED benefits from premium product and styling assets that naturally suit visual-first browsing.
Maintain a consistent posting frequency to keep the official account active and relevant.
Optimize the WeChat menu structure to improve access to brand updates, product drops, store information and community content.
Leverage brand campaign/moment to activate engagement campaigns to build a stronger brand hub.
Expand content formats beyond product and styling visuals to build a richer brand narrative.
Develop clear content columns with consistent naming, so new users can quickly understand and browse Malbon’s key content areas.

Product and brand discovery and review.
Used as a dynamic info search engine.

“She-economy”: 70% active female users.
No.1 platform for creator marketing.
High purchasing power.
Good for celebrity collaboration, but engagement is declining.

Good for mass targeting to grow awareness.
Requires frequent livestreaming to perform.
RED should be the core platform to build discoverability, creator credibility and purchase intent.
Channel suggestion: lean more resources on RED for brand building and performance-driven actions.
We plan six content pillars to build Malbon’s always-on social narrative. Each pillar gives the brand account a clear content territory.

Behind the drop
Brand-oriented content can make the brand easier to understand through lighter, more human storytelling.
The 6 posts under @HONMA’s brand story collection generated 120K+ total engagements, with 5 out of 6 posts receiving over 1K likes and becoming viral posts.
@MARIMEKKO used an office vlog format to show the brand’s HQ culture, turning internal daily moments into approachable brand storytelling and helping audiences feel closer to the brand.
Moodboard-style content is a natural extension of Malbon’s origin, helping the brand continuously express its taste, attitude and values in a softer way.
@AMI Paris curated playlist has received 3K+ listens, while the hashtag #AmiParis友你真好 generated around 500K views.
@Sporty & Rich uses playlists, moodboards and short statements to softly express its lifestyle values, attracting audiences who identify with the same taste, attitude and cultural references.
A strong product drop needs a full storyline from teaser to styling scenario to build product desire progressively.

rows: New Collection Launch: use PDPAOLA ICONS & ROUJE PALOMA as examples

paragraphs.text: Build pre-launch curiosity through product details, mood visuals and short campaign snippets.
Feature global campaign assets with platform-friendly captions and content sequencing, helping audiences better understand the collection.
Feature Malbon team narration on design inspiration, functional details, material choices and golf culture references.
Use KOL content to show the collection across different scenarios, from course to city life.
Signature brand elements can be turned into reusable content assets to strengthen recognition and engagement.
The Bucket character can become a recognizable social cue, helping Malbon build a more ownable and playful brand language across RED and WeChat.

@MARIMEKKO turned its iconic prints into social assets across wallpapers, calendars and behind-the-scenes content, including print mill stories and factory visit videos. These formats helped the brand repeatedly reinforce its print heritage while creating useful, save-worthy and highly engaging content.
Collaboration content should turn both brands’ audiences into shared growth opportunities through co-created assets, mutual tagging, shared hashtags and paid amplification.
Marimekko x CASETiFY used co-created print assets, mutual mentions and shared product links to connect both brand audiences and create multiple discovery entry points for the collaboration.
Special occasions and collaborations are the most effective moments to activate engagement campaigns
Spread the love—tag your special someone and comment below for a chance to win this gift
Marimekko x HEYTEA Collaboration Engagement Campaign
Invited users to share photos and stories of their encounters with Marimekko and HEYTEA for a chance to win a limited gift box.
Most Western Golf Brands Are Buying Impressions. None of Them Are Building Identity

Most golf brands today focus only on golf green storytelling, with limited creator diversity and highly repetitive content. This fails to build consumer connection with the brand and further reinforces stereotypes around golf culture.
Break Out of the Golf Bubble
Cooperated Creators should not function only as clothing models, but as expressions of the brand’s style and identity, helping audiences form the right associations with the brand.

Maintain golf culture storytelling while moving beyond traditional green-focused narratives and overly commercial creator content. Reframe golf culture through a more connected, relaxed, and modern storytelling approach.
From Influencers to Brand Insiders — Building a Creator Community
One campaign buys attention. A community builds a brand.
Paid Campaign — amplify what's already real
Offline Events — turn followers into a circle
Here's what this looks like when it compounds over time
By the end of 2025, we had help Marimekko developed over 50 high-potential creators for long-term partnerships.
Creators with partnerships lasting over one year tend to deliver stronger sales performance during key promotional periods.
We have a powerful fashion KOL network for in-depth collaboration
Over 5,670 KOL for premium/high fashion industry
Detail profile including the style, promotion value, and e-commerce conversion capability
Strong personal connection with over 1,500 influencers for in-depth collaboration
Tracking beyond social indicators: Mini Program visits, Tmall search traffic, add to cart
Deep collaboration with media buy team to maxims impact of campaign
Provide customized optimization suggestions including additional sharing within KOL’s private groups
We structure creator partnerships in tiers — matching investment level to audience scale and content role

paragraphs.text: Continuously engage high-potential lifestyle audiences via in-feeds ads and efficiently expand beyond the core community through offline activations
Drive concentrated brand exposure in a short period of time through brand advertising via WeChat Moments and RED Branding Banner
Continuously through targeted lifestyle audience, turning potential users into part of the Malbon community
Gain free exposure by seeding high-potential influencers, and amplify product visibility through reposts on official accounts supported by RED ads.
Reposting on OA and Sales Account
Based on the brand’s annual product launch plan, we recommend developing tailored, theme-based campaigns around key collection launches as important brand moments.
Localized global campaign strategy into China-specific creative direction, including concept development for official campaign assets such as product and model visuals
Designed integrated influencer and paid media strategies to support both brand storytelling and performance objectives
Enabled a full-funnel marketing approach, driving seamless conversion from awareness to purchase through coordinated content and media execution

# of store visits coming from Red

ROI from Xiaohongxing varies for different industries:
Beyond key brand moments, we recommend localized campaigns for China-specific festivals, grounded in consumer insights to avoid generic global brand content.
Case Study: SIGMA 2026 New Year Campaign

Leveraging the product’s camera features and consumer behaviors around gifting and memory-capturing during the New Year, we developed three creator-led storytelling themes focused on capturing moments with self, family, and friends.
Case Study: Missoma 2026 Qixi Valentine’s Day Campaign

Break away from the traditional couple-only Valentine’s narrative by including maternal love, highlighting that care extends beyond partners to children. Anchored in the “holding hands” design detail, the story emphasizes support and interdependence to express the brand’s values.
Collaborate with innovative and individual content creators to create a Malbon community. Focus on sponsored KOL campaigns during big brand brand moments and peak sales seasons.
